requirement.original: Fix the LOCK_FILE EISDIR stderr leak in hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh at line 129. Change `} 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null` to `} 2>/dev/null 9> "$LOCK_FILE"`. Add AC7c to cover this path.
requirement.clarified: Single-line redirect reorder at hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh:129 to silence LOCK_FILE EISDIR stderr leak, plus AC7c test for the LOCK_FILE-as-directory error path.
requirement.what: Reorder Bash redirects on line 129 from `} 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null` to `} 2>/dev/null 9> "$LOCK_FILE"`
requirement.why: Bash evaluates redirections left-to-right; placing `9> "$LOCK_FILE"` before `2>/dev/null` means the EISDIR open(2) failure emits to the real stderr before the null-redirect takes effect, leaking one line of error output.

excluded: AC7 / AC7b tests (must not be regressed)
arch-7 phase 2 or any other spec item

success_criteria: AC7c harness: LOCK_FILE pre-created as dir, hook exits rc=0 stdout='' stderr=''
AC7 and AC7b continue to pass
constraints: Only line 129 changes; no other lines touched
Do not bundle with arch-7 phase 2 or any other pending item

root_cause_analysis.symptom: When LOCK_FILE (`${COUNTER_FILE}.lock`) is pre-created as a directory, the hook leaks one stderr line instead of remaining completely silent.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Bash redirect ordering bug at line 129: `} 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null` places the fd-9 open before the stderr redirection. Bash processes redirections left-to-right, so the `9>` open(2) syscall fails with EISDIR and Bash emits the error to file-descriptor 2 (still the real stderr) before `2>/dev/null` is applied.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — this is a pre-existing ordering error in the original implementation; no regression commit to bisect
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The flock brace-group pattern appends redirections in the order `9>` (lock fd) then `2>/dev/null` (silence). The COUNTER_FILE EISDIR fix in Cycle 3 correctly placed `2>/dev/null` before `>` for the inner writes but did not apply the same principle to the outer brace-group closing line.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: The hook contract is `stderr=''` unconditionally (non-blocking hook). Any EISDIR stderr leak violates this contract.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Present before Cycle 3; identified as out-of-scope for Cycle 3; promoted to in-scope for Cycle 4 micro-fix.
observations: line 129 text `} 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null` confirmed by Read tool (offset 120, line 129)

comment at lines 116-117 documents the correct principle (`2>/dev/null placed before > so EISDIR stderr is suppressed before the output redirect is evaluated`) — the outer brace-group line contradicts this comment (inferred from source read)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — no runtime reproduction performed; the ordering principle is deterministic Bash specification behaviour)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Read tool confirmed line 129 contains `} 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null`; existing comment at lines 116-117 documents the correct left-to-right ordering principle and shows the inner writes already apply it correctly.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — not a regression; original implementation error identified in spec review
